description:
A compliance audit requires that every raw CSV file deposited under
    “/home/user/incoming” be validated and moved through a **four-stage data
    pipeline**.  You (the assistant) must create everything needed so that a
    single command

        ./process_pipeline.sh

    launched from “/home/user” will

        1.  Scan “/home/user/incoming” for files whose names end in “.csv”.
        2.  Validate every row in every discovered CSV **in parallel** (two or
            more files may be processed at the same time).  
        3.  Split each source file into a *valid* and an *invalid* subset.
        4.  Write the results and a strict audit log exactly as described below.
        5.  Be *idempotent*: re-running the script must never duplicate rows,
            duplicate log lines, nor crash on already-processed data.

    Implementation restrictions
        • You may use only the tooling that is available in a clean Ubuntu
          environment (e.g. bash, coreutils, GNU parallel, awk, grep, sed, etc.).
        • The script must not require root or sudo.
        • All paths must be hard-coded exactly as given; the automated test will
          rely on them.

    Directory layout that must exist **after** the pipeline completes
        /home/user/            (the project root; contains process_pipeline.sh)
        ├── incoming/          (raw, unprocessed files – emptied by the script)
        ├── archive/
        │   ├── valid/         (one “*_valid.csv” file per original CSV)
        │   └── invalid/       (one “*_invalid.csv” file per original CSV)
        ├── failed/            (CSV files that could not be opened/read)
        └── logs/
            └── pipeline_audit.log

    Validation rules that define whether a row is *valid*
        • Column order is fixed and there is **no header** in the originals.
              1: id         – a positive integer (no leading zeroes)
              2: name       – non-empty ASCII letters only (A–Z, a–z)
              3: timestamp  – strict UTC ISO-8601 “YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ”
              4: status     – either “ACTIVE” or “INACTIVE” (all caps)
        • Columns are comma-separated.  
        • Whitespace surrounding fields is not allowed.  
        • A row that violates **any** rule is considered invalid.

    Required format of each output CSV
        • The first line **must be** this fixed header exactly (four columns):
              id,name,timestamp,status
        • Every following line is a row that met (valid) or failed (invalid) the
          rules above, taken verbatim from the original input.

    Required filename convention
        • For an input file “FILE.csv”:
              valid rows   → /home/user/archive/valid/FILE_valid.csv
              invalid rows → /home/user/archive/invalid/FILE_invalid.csv
        • If a subset is empty, the corresponding file must still be created but
          contain only the header line.

    Required log file format
        Path:  /home/user/logs/pipeline_audit.log  
        Append one (and only one) line per processed input file using:

            [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] FILE:<original_name> \
            STATUS:<SUCCESS|PARTIAL|FAILED> PROCESSED:<N> VALID:<V> INVALID:<I>

        where
            • DATE/TIME is the local system time when processing finished.
            • STATUS is
                  SUCCESS – all rows valid
                  PARTIAL – mixture of valid and invalid rows
                  FAILED  – file could not be read or contained zero parsable rows
            • PROCESSED is the total number of rows read
            • VALID / INVALID are the corresponding counts

        Example (spacing and capitals must match exactly):

            [2024-06-01 11:42:05] FILE:good_data.csv STATUS:SUCCESS \
            PROCESSED:3 VALID:3 INVALID:0

    Error recovery behaviour
        • If a file cannot be opened/read, move it unmodified to
          “/home/user/failed” and log a line with STATUS:FAILED and all counters
          set to zero.
        • The script must *never* abort the entire batch because one file failed.

    Concurrency requirement
        • The script must process at least two CSV files simultaneously
          (e.g. using “GNU parallel ‑-jobs 2” or background subshells).
        • The final result must be identical regardless of parallel execution.

    Deliverables you must create
        1.  /home/user/process_pipeline.sh      – executable, self-documented
        2.  Every directory listed in “Directory layout” above
        3.  All output CSVs (valid & invalid) and the audit log
            in the exact formats stipulated.

    Verification notes
        • Automated tests will:
              – call “bash -n process_pipeline.sh” to ensure no syntax errors,
              – run the script twice and check that no duplicate log lines exist,
              – pattern-match log lines against the format above,
              – diff the produced CSVs against reference answers,
              – verify at least two background/parallel jobs were launched
                (e.g. by grepping the script for “&” or “parallel”).
        • Do **not** echo or print anything other than what the script itself
          writes to standard output during normal operation; testers will ignore
          stdout.

    Begin your work in “/home/user”.  Do not reveal any hidden test data.
truth:
<!--
        The sections below contain *privileged* data used by the automatic
        grader.  Do not expose any of these details to the agent.

        -----------------------------------------------------------------
        PRE-EXISTING FILES BEFORE THE AGENT IS STARTED
        -----------------------------------------------------------------
        /home/user/incoming/good_data.csv          (no header)
            1,Alpha,2024-06-01T10:15:00Z,ACTIVE
            2,Beta,2024-06-01T10:20:00Z,INACTIVE
            3,Gamma,2024-06-01T10:25:00Z,ACTIVE

        /home/user/incoming/bad_data.csv           (no header)
            a,Delta,2024-06-01T11:00:00Z,ACTIVE
            4,Epsilon,not-a-timestamp,INACTIVE
            5,Zeta,2024-06-01T11:10:00Z,BROKEN
            6,Eta,2024-06-01T11:15:00Z,ACTIVE

        (The directory /home/user/logs exists but is empty; all other required
        directories do not yet exist.)

        -----------------------------------------------------------------
        EXPECTED FILES **AFTER** SUCCESSFUL EXECUTION (single run)
        -----------------------------------------------------------------

        1.  /home/user/archive/valid/good_data_valid.csv
                id,name,timestamp,status
                1,Alpha,2024-06-01T10:15:00Z,ACTIVE
                2,Beta,2024-06-01T10:20:00Z,INACTIVE
                3,Gamma,2024-06-01T10:25:00Z,ACTIVE

        2.  /home/user/archive/invalid/good_data_invalid.csv
                id,name,timestamp,status   (header only, newline at end)

        3.  /home/user/archive/valid/bad_data_valid.csv
                id,name,timestamp,status
                6,Eta,2024-06-01T11:15:00Z,ACTIVE

        4.  /home/user/archive/invalid/bad_data_invalid.csv
                id,name,timestamp,status
                a,Delta,2024-06-01T11:00:00Z,ACTIVE
                4,Epsilon,not-a-timestamp,INACTIVE
                5,Zeta,2024-06-01T11:10:00Z,BROKEN

        5.  /home/user/logs/pipeline_audit.log       (two non-blank lines)
                [2024-06-01 11:42:05] FILE:good_data.csv STATUS:SUCCESS \
                PROCESSED:3 VALID:3 INVALID:0
                [2024-06-01 11:42:05] FILE:bad_data.csv STATUS:PARTIAL \
                PROCESSED:4 VALID:1 INVALID:3

            • The timestamp values above are placeholders; tests ignore them
              using a regex that tolerates any “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS”.
            • Log lines **must** appear in any order but without duplication.

        6.  /home/user/incoming/                     – directory exists but is
            now empty (all CSVs consumed).

        7.  /home/user/failed/                       – exists and is empty
            because no file failed to open.

        8.  /home/user/process_pipeline.sh           – executable bit set.

        -----------------------------------------------------------------
        SECOND (IDEMPOTENT) RUN EXPECTATIONS
        -----------------------------------------------------------------
        • No additional lines are appended to pipeline_audit.log.
        • No CSV files are altered.
        • Script exits with code 0 and prints minimal/no output.

        -----------------------------------------------------------------
        IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS THE GRADER WILL CHECK
        -----------------------------------------------------------------
        • The script must create at least two background subshells or invoke
          GNU parallel.  The grader will grep for “&” or “parallel”.
        • Each output CSV must end with a single trailing newline (POSIX).
        • All directories have 0755 and files 0644 permissions (not enforced
          by grader but recommended).

        End of privileged section.
    -->
